mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is below 1% and stable, discount rates stay compressed and equity risk premia dominate, so S&P 500 tends to rise over the next month as long as inflation does not reaccelerate sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of months with FEDFUNDS below 1% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation not accelerating, or if rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and future demand, so SPX forward returns turn negative over the next month once unemployment breaks higher by a meaningful margin.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if PAYEMS does not weaken alongside the unemployment rise in at least two-thirds of triggers.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broadening domestic demand, which lifts earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising HOUST fails to deliver positive 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL growth stays contained, firms are expanding real output without margin pressure, so equities should outperform over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3m annualized inflation < 3% and INDPRO rises
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases do not show positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or if high INDPRO with low inflation still coincides with negative returns for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening surprise in FEDFUNDS above 4.5% raises the discount rate and usually pressures valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are non-negative in more than 60% of months after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or if the signal reverses sign when rates are already elevated.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth that remains positive while unemployment is falling confirms resilient labor income and stabilizes spending, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with rising payrolls and falling unemployment do not outperform baseline on 20d SPX returns, or if the sign becomes negative when PAYEMS growth is strongest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals demand destruction in housing and related consumer durables, which should weigh on cyclicals and push the index lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if housing decline triggers are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or if RSAFS remains strong and fully offsets the housing signal.

mechanism_narrative: If INDPRO weakens while PAYEMS also decelerates, the economy is entering a broad production slowdown rather than a healthy inventory reset, so SPX should fall over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in industrial production and payrolls does not lead to negative 20d SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the effect disappears once one series is controlled for independently.
